# üß¨‚ú® Tidy WiGiTS Outputs

[![conda-latest1](https://anaconda.org/tidywf/r-tidywigits/badges/latest_release_date.svg "Conda Latest Release")](https://anaconda.org/tidywf/r-tidywigits)
[![gha](https://github.com/tidywf/tidywigits/actions/workflows/deploy.yaml/badge.svg "GitHub Actions")](https://github.com/tidywf/tidywigits/actions/workflows/deploy.yaml)

- üìö Docs: <https://tidywf.github.io/tidywigits>:
  - [Installation](https://tidywf.github.io/tidywigits/articles/installation)
  - [Files/tables
    supported](https://tidywf.github.io/tidywigits/articles/schemas_raw)
  - [Tidy
    schemas](https://tidywf.github.io/tidywigits/articles/schemas_tidy)
  - [Developer
    notes](https://tidywf.github.io/tidywigits/articles/developers)
  - [Changelog](https://tidywf.github.io/tidywigits/articles/NEWS)

## Overview

{tidywigits} is an R package that parses and tidies outputs from the
[WiGiTS](https://github.com/hartwigmedical/hmftools "WiGiTS suite")
suite of genome and transcriptome analysis tools for cancer research and
diagnostics, created by the Hartwig Medical Foundation.

In short, it traverses through a directory containing results from one
or more runs of WiGiTS tools, parses any files it recognises, tidies
them up (which includes data reshaping, normalisation, column name
cleanup etc.), and writes them to the output format of choice
e.g.¬ÝApache Parquet, PostgreSQL, TSV, RDS.

## üé® Quick Start

The starting point of {tidywigits} is a directory with WiGiTS results.
Let‚Äôs look at some sample data (tracked via [DVC](https://dvc.org/))
under <https://github.com/tidywf/tidywigits/tree/main/inst/extdata/oa>:

Click here

``` r
system.file("extdata/oa", package = "tidywigits") |>
  fs::dir_tree(invert = TRUE, glob = "*.dvc")
/home/runner/miniconda3/envs/pkgdown_env/lib/R/library/tidywigits/extdata/oa
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ alignments
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.duplicate_freq.tsv
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ amber
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.amber.baf.pcf
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.amber.contamination.tsv
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.amber.homozygousregion.tsv
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.amber.qc
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ bamtools
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.bam_metric.coverage.tsv
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.bam_metric.exon_medians.tsv
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.bam_metric.flag_counts.tsv
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.bam_metric.frag_length.tsv
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.bam_metric.gene_coverage.tsv
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.bam_metric.partition_stats.tsv
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.bam_metric.summary.tsv
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.wgsmetrics
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ chord
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.chord.mutation_contexts.tsv
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.chord.prediction.tsv
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ cider
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.cider.blastn_match.tsv.gz
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.cider.locus_stats.tsv
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.cider.vdj.tsv.gz
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ cobalt
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ cobalt.version
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.cobalt.gc.median.tsv
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.cobalt.ratio.median.tsv
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.cobalt.ratio.pcf
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ cuppa
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.cuppa.pred_summ.tsv
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.cuppa.vis_data.tsv
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.cuppa_data.tsv.gz
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ esvee
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.esvee.alignment.tsv
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.esvee.assembly.tsv

‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.esvee.phased_assembly.tsv

‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.esvee.prep.fragment_length.tsv
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.esvee.prep.junction.tsv
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ flagstats
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.flagstat
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ isofox
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.isf.alt_splice_junc.csv
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.isf.fusions.csv
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.isf.gene_collection.csv
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.isf.gene_data.csv
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.isf.pass_fusions.csv
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.isf.retained_intron.csv
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.isf.summary.csv
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.isf.transcript_data.csv
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ lilac
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.lilac.candidates.coverage.tsv
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.lilac.qc.tsv
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.lilac.tsv
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ linx
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ germline_annotations
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ linx.version
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.linx.germline.breakend.tsv
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.linx.germline.clusters.tsv
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.linx.germline.disruption.tsv
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.linx.germline.driver.catalog.tsv
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.linx.germline.links.tsv
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.linx.germline.svs.tsv
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ somatic_annotations
‚îÇ       ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ linx.version
‚îÇ       ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.linx.breakend.tsv
‚îÇ       ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.linx.clusters.tsv
‚îÇ       ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.linx.driver.catalog.tsv
‚îÇ       ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.linx.drivers.tsv
‚îÇ       ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.linx.fusion.tsv
‚îÇ       ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.linx.links.tsv
‚îÇ       ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.linx.svs.tsv
‚îÇ       ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.linx.vis_copy_number.tsv

‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.peach.qc.tsv

‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.sig.allocation.tsv
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.sig.snv_counts.csv
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ teal
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.teal.breakend.tsv.gz
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.teal.tellength.tsv
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ virusbreakend
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.virusbreakend.vcf.summary.tsv
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ virusinterpreter
    ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ sample1.virus.annotated.tsv
```

We can parse, tidy up, and write the WiGiTS results into e.g.¬ÝParquet
format or a PostgreSQL database as follows:

- Parquet:

``` r
in_dir <- system.file("extdata/oa", package = "tidywigits")
out_dir <- tempdir() |> fs::dir_create("parquet_example")
w <- Wigits$new(in_dir)
res <- w$nemofy(diro = out_dir, format = "parquet", input_id = "parquet_example")
fs::dir_info(out_dir) |>
  dplyr::mutate(bname = basename(.data$path)) |>
  dplyr::select("bname", "size", "type")
# A tibble: 95 √ó 3
   bname                                         size type 
   <chr>                                  <fs::bytes> <fct>
 1 metadata.json                               12.33K file 
 2 sample1_2_sage_bqrtsv.parquet                4.19K file 
 3 sample1_alignments_dupfreq.parquet           2.86K file 
 4 sample1_amber_bafpcf.parquet                 4.34K file 
 5 sample1_amber_contaminationtsv.parquet        5.3K file 
 6 sample1_amber_homozygousregion.parquet       4.26K file 
 7 sample1_amber_qc.parquet                     3.29K file 
 8 sample1_bamtools_coverage.parquet            2.66K file 
 9 sample1_bamtools_exoncvg.parquet             3.98K file 
10 sample1_bamtools_flagstats.parquet           7.58K file 
# ‚Ñπ 85 more rows
```

- PostgreSQL:

``` r
in_dir <- system.file("extdata/oa", package = "tidywigits")
out_dir <- tempdir() |> fs::dir_create("parquet_example")
w <- Wigits$new(in_dir)
dbconn <- DBI::dbConnect(
  drv = RPostgres::Postgres(),
  dbname = "nemo",
  user = "orcabus"
)
res <- w$nemofy(
  format = "db",
  input_id = "db_example",
  dbconn = dbconn
)
```

**IMPORTANT**: support for VCFs is still under development.

## üçï Installation

Using {remotes} directly from GitHub:

``` r
install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("tidywf/tidywigits") # latest main commit
remotes::install_github("tidywf/tidywigits@v0.0.7.9001") # released version
```

Alternatively:

- conda package: <https://anaconda.org/tidywf/r-tidywigits>
- Docker image:
  <https://github.com/tidywf/tidywigits/pkgs/container/tidywigits>

For more details see:
<https://tidywf.github.io/tidywigits/articles/installation>

## üåÄ CLI

A `tidywigits.R` command line interface is available for convenience.

- If you‚Äôre using the conda package, the `tidywigits.R` command will
  already be available inside the activated conda environment.
- If you‚Äôre *not* using the conda package, you need to export the
  `tidywigits/inst/cli/` directory to your `PATH` in order to use
  `tidywigits.R`.

``` bash
tw_cli=$(Rscript -e 'x = system.file("cli", package = "tidywigits"); cat(x, "\n")' | xargs)
export PATH="${tw_cli}:${PATH}"
```

``` R
$ tidywigits.R --version
tidywigits 0.0.7.9000

#-----------------------------------#
$ tidywigits.R --help
usage: tidywigits.R [-h] [-v] {tidy,list} ...

‚ú® WiGiTS Output Tidying ‚ú®

positional arguments:
  {tidy,list}    sub-command help
    tidy         Tidy Workflow Outputs
    list         List Parsable Workflow Outputs

options:
  -h, --help     show this help message and exit
  -v, --version  show program's version number and exit
'
#-----------------------------------#
#------- Tidy ----------------------#
$ tidywigits.R tidy --help
usage: tidywigits.R tidy [-h] -d IN_DIR [-o OUT_DIR] [-f FORMAT] -i ID
                         [--dbname DBNAME] [--dbuser DBUSER]
                         [--include INCLUDE] [--exclude EXCLUDE] [-q]

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  -d, --in_dir IN_DIR   Input directory.
  -o, --out_dir OUT_DIR
                        Output directory.
  -f, --format FORMAT   Format of output [def: parquet] (parquet, db, tsv,
                        csv, rds)
  -i, --id ID           ID to use for this run.
  --dbname DBNAME       Database name.
  --dbuser DBUSER       Database user.
  --include INCLUDE     Include only these files (comma,sep).
  --exclude EXCLUDE     Exclude these files (comma,sep).
  -q, --quiet           Shush all the logs.

#-----------------------------------#
#------- List ----------------------#
$ tidywigits.R list --help
usage: tidywigits.R list [-h] -d IN_DIR [-f FORMAT] [-q]

options:
  -h, --help           show this help message and exit
  -d, --in_dir IN_DIR  Input directory.
  -f, --format FORMAT  Format of list output [def: pretty] (tsv, pretty)
  -q, --quiet          Shush all the logs.
```

# Package index

## Tool Classes

- [`Alignments`](https://tidywf.github.io/tidywigits/dev/reference/Alignments.md)
  : Alignments Object
- [`Amber`](https://tidywf.github.io/tidywigits/dev/reference/Amber.md)
  : Amber Object
- [`Bamtools`](https://tidywf.github.io/tidywigits/dev/reference/Bamtools.md)
  : Bamtools Object
- [`Chord`](https://tidywf.github.io/tidywigits/dev/reference/Chord.md)
  : Chord Object
- [`Cider`](https://tidywf.github.io/tidywigits/dev/reference/Cider.md)
  : Cider Object
- [`Cobalt`](https://tidywf.github.io/tidywigits/dev/reference/Cobalt.md)
  : Cobalt Object
- [`Cuppa`](https://tidywf.github.io/tidywigits/dev/reference/Cuppa.md)
  : Cuppa Object
- [`Esvee`](https://tidywf.github.io/tidywigits/dev/reference/Esvee.md)
  : Esvee Object
- [`Flagstats`](https://tidywf.github.io/tidywigits/dev/reference/Flagstats.md)
  : Flagstats Object
- [`Isofox`](https://tidywf.github.io/tidywigits/dev/reference/Isofox.md)
  : Isofox Object
- [`Lilac`](https://tidywf.github.io/tidywigits/dev/reference/Lilac.md)
  : Lilac Object
- [`Linx`](https://tidywf.github.io/tidywigits/dev/reference/Linx.md) :
  Linx Object
- [`Neo`](https://tidywf.github.io/tidywigits/dev/reference/Neo.md) :
  Neo Object
- [`Peach`](https://tidywf.github.io/tidywigits/dev/reference/Peach.md)
  : Peach Object
- [`Purple`](https://tidywf.github.io/tidywigits/dev/reference/Purple.md)
  : Purple Object
- [`Sage`](https://tidywf.github.io/tidywigits/dev/reference/Sage.md) :
  Sage Object
- [`Sigs`](https://tidywf.github.io/tidywigits/dev/reference/Sigs.md) :
  Sigs Object
- [`Teal`](https://tidywf.github.io/tidywigits/dev/reference/Teal.md) :
  Teal Object
- [`Virusbreakend`](https://tidywf.github.io/tidywigits/dev/reference/Virusbreakend.md)
  : Virusbreakend Object
- [`Virusinterpreter`](https://tidywf.github.io/tidywigits/dev/reference/Virusinterpreter.md)
  : Virusinterpreter Object

## Workflows

- [`Wigits`](https://tidywf.github.io/tidywigits/dev/reference/Wigits.md)
  : Wigits Object

## Other

- [`WIGITS_TOOLS`](https://tidywf.github.io/tidywigits/dev/reference/WIGITS_TOOLS.md)
  : WiGiTS Tool Names
- [`purple_plot_getter()`](https://tidywf.github.io/tidywigits/dev/reference/purple_plot_getter.md)
  : Retrieve PURPLE Plots
- [`s3sync()`](https://tidywf.github.io/tidywigits/dev/reference/s3sync.md)
  : AWS S3 Sync Helper
